_instructions.critical: Only modify 'passes' field. Never change structure.
_instructions.workflow: One task at a time. Full verification. Commit after each.

meta.title: React Compiler Errors + Unused Variables Cleanup (Round 2)

targets.EventTimeline.tsx: Line 140 - Cannot call impure function (Date.now) during render
targets.SentimentTrendChart.tsx: Line 165 - Cannot create components (CustomTooltip) during render
targets.PaperTradePerformance.tsx: Line 194 - Cannot reassign variable after render
targets.ProfileSelector.tsx: Lines 219, 230 - Cannot create components (Wrapper) during render
targets.LogsCard.tsx: Line 115 - Memoization could not be preserved

tasks.description: Fix EventTimeline.tsx Date.now() call in useMemo
steps: Line 140: Date.now() is impure - called during useMemo computation
Move 'const now = Date.now()' OUTSIDE useMemo, pass as dependency
OR use useRef to cache the timestamp if it shouldn't change
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Line 165: <Tooltip content={<CustomTooltip />} /> creates component during render
Move CustomTooltip component definition OUTSIDE the main component
Ensure it's a stable reference, not re-created each render
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Line 194: 'cumulative += trade.returnPct' reassigns after render
Wrap chart data computation in useMemo
Move the closedTrades filter and chartData map into a single useMemo
Use reduce instead of mutable cumulative variable
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Lines 208-215: Wrapper component defined inside render function
Move Wrapper outside ProfileSelector as standalone component
Accept variant, className, children as props
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Lines 89-109: useEffect syncing props to state is anti-pattern
Use lazy initialization: useState(() => preferences.value)
Remove the sync effect entirely
OR use a key prop to reset component when preferences change
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Line 115: Memoization could not be preserved
Check if useMemo dependencies are stable
Ensure sortOrder is memoized or stable
May need to restructure the computation
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
Lines 288-289: setState calls in effect for empty items
Derive changedCells/recentlyUpdatedRows from items instead
Use useMemo to compute changed state based on items
OR use conditional early return without setState
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer errors
SeedEvolution.tsx:23 - Remove unused statusIcons
PaperTradesTable.tsx:91 - Remove unused getRiskBadgeVariant
AddSymbolModal.tsx:94 - Remove unused reject param or use _reject
ExpandedRowNarrative.tsx:26 - Remove unused _item param
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer warnings
ReadinessIndicator.tsx:18 - Remove unused symbol
SparklineWithHistory.tsx:45 - Remove unused desiredDays
WatchlistTable.tsx:221,227 - Remove unused getNewsBadgeVariant, formatNewsScore
WatchlistTable.tsx:439-442 - Remove unused priceScore, techScore, priceStale, techStale
Verify: npm run lint shows fewer warnings
Run: cd frontend && npm run build
Run: cd frontend && npm run lint (expect 0 errors)
Run: cd backend && .venv/bin/pytest tests/unit tests/analytics -x --tb=short -q
Verify: bash ~/portfolio-ai/scripts/restart.sh succeeds
Verify: curl -sf http://localhost:8000/api/health returns healthy
